fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Dakota Pharmacy Technician License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to North Dakota - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacy technician license reciprocity north dakota, how to transfer pharmacy technician license to north dakota, pharmacy technician compact north dakota, north dakota pharmacy technician endorsement application

board.name: North Dakota Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
North Dakota Board of Pharmacy
1906 East Broadway Avenue
Bismarck, ND 58501-1335

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 12 hours per renewal cycle, including 1 hour of pharmacy law and 1 hour of patient safety/error prevention

license_types.title: Certified Pharmacy Technician (CPhT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacy technician in North Dakota.

steps.title: Review Licensure Requirements
steps.description: Visit the North Dakota Board of Pharmacy's 'Technicians' page (nodakpharmacy.com/technicians) and review the 'Instructions for New Technicians' to understand all eligibility criteria, including age, education, character, and the 18-month requirement for certification and training.

steps.title: Download and Complete Application
steps.description: Download the 'Pharmacy Technician Application (NEW)' form from the Board's website at nodakpharmacy.com/technicians/pharmacy-technician-application. Complete all sections of the application accurately.

steps.title: Gather Supporting Documents
steps.description: Collect all required supporting documents as outlined in the application form and instructions. This typically includes proof of age, high school diploma/GED, and the Criminal History Record Check Authorization form.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fee
steps.description: Mail the completed application form, all supporting documents, and the non-refundable application fee of $35.00 (check or money order payable to 'ND Board of Pharmacy') to the Board's mailing address: North Dakota Board of Pharmacy, 1906 East Broadway Avenue, Bismarck, ND 58501-1335.

steps.title: Complete Certification and Training
steps.description: Within 18 months of initial licensure, you must successfully pass a Board-approved certification exam (PTCB or NHA ExCPT) and complete a Board-approved training program. Evidence of both must be submitted to the Board.

steps.title: Maintain Licensure
steps.description: After initial licensure, adhere to all renewal requirements, including continuing education, as stipulated by the North Dakota Board of Pharmacy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the PTCB. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a North Dakota pharmacy technician license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does North Dakota have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in North Dakota. To work in North Dakota, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to North Dakota?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Dakota while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacy Technicians licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacy Technician in North Dakota
hero.dek: Apply for a Pharmacy Technician license in North Dakota by downloading the application form from the North Dakota Board of Pharmacy's website (nodakpharmacy.com/technicians/pharmacy-technician-application), completing it, and submitting it via mail.

official_links.portal_name: North Dakota Board of Pharmacy Paper Application
official_links.registration_steps: There is no online application portal for initial licensure. Applicants must download the application form from the North Dakota Board of Pharmacy's website, complete it, and submit it via mail with all required documents and payment.
